full_response:
Let  

S ≡ U_a V^b g_{ab}                                               (1)

We want to show that S has the same numerical value in every inertial frame that is related to another by a Lorentz transformation Λ.

1. Transformation rules  

Unprimed → primed frame:

• Contravariant vector         U'^μ = Λ^μ_{ ν} U^ν ,  V'^μ = Λ^μ_{ ν} V^ν                     (2)

• Covariant vector (lowered with the metric)  
  U'_α = g'_{αβ} U'^β  
         = g'_{αβ} Λ^β_{ μ} U^μ                                                            (3)

• Metric tensor (covariant) transforms as  

g'_{αβ} = Λ_{α}^{ ρ} Λ_{β}^{ σ} g_{ρσ}                                                 (4)

Here and below repeated indices are summed, and indices are raised/lowered with the Minkowski metric g_{ab} (≡ η_{ab}).  We also use the mixed-index matrices

Λ_{α}^{ ρ} ≡ g_{αμ} Λ^μ_{ ν} g^{νρ}.                                                       (5)

Because Λ is Lorentz, it satisfies the orthogonality relation  

Λ^T g Λ = g  ⇔  g_{αβ} Λ^α_{ μ} Λ^β_{ ν} = g_{μν} ⇔  Λ_{α}^{ μ} Λ^{α}_{ ν} = δ^{μ}_{ ν}.   (6)

2. The quantity in the primed frame  

S' ≡ U'_α V'^β g'_{αβ}.                                                                    (7)

Insert the transformation laws (2)–(4):

S' = (Λ_{α}^{ μ} U_μ)(Λ^{β}_{ ν} V^ν)(Λ_{α}^{ ρ} Λ_{β}^{ σ} g_{ρσ}).                  (8)

3. Use orthogonality of Λ  

First contract the two Λ’s that carry the same lowered index α:

Λ_{α}^{ μ} Λ_{α}^{ ρ} = Λ_{α}^{ μ} g^{αβ} g_{βγ} Λ^{γρ} = δ^{μ}_{ ρ}.                (9)

Likewise for the β index:

Λ^{β}_{ ν} Λ_{β}^{ σ} = δ^{σ}_{ ν}.                                                    (10)

With these identities, (8) simplifies to

S' = U_μ V^ν δ^{μ}_{ ρ} δ^{σ}_{ ν} g_{ρσ}  
  = U_ρ V^σ g_{ρσ}.                                                                  (11)

4. Relabel dummy indices (ρ → a, σ → b) and compare with (1):

S' = U_a V^b g_{ab} = S.                                                                (12)

Therefore the contraction U_a V^b g_{ab} is invariant under Lorentz transformations; i.e. it is a Lorentz scalar.

\boxed{\,U_a V^b g_{ab}\; \text{is a Lorentz scalar:}\; U'_α V'^β g'_{αβ}=U_a V^b g_{ab}\,}

Confidence: 96%
extracted_answer: \,U_a V^b g_{ab}\; \text{is a Lorentz scalar:}\; U'_α V'^β g'_{αβ}=U_a V^b g_{ab}\,
